responses.text: The suicide rate in children and adolescents under the age of 20 increased by 49% during the second wave of the epidemic.
Japan's Prime Minister Yoshihide Suga announced the creation of a new "minister of loneliness" in his cabinet to address Japan's record-breaking suicide rate.
About 40% of suicide prevention organizations in Japan have temporarily closed or reduced their working hours due to the epidemic
The epidemic has exacerbated women's worries about work, childcare, care and domestic violence, according to the Ministry of Health, Labor and Welfare.
A large number of women are unemployed during the epidemic, with 280,000, 140,000, and 110,000 fewer women working in the hotel, restaurant, and wholesale and retail industries respectively.
The COVID-19 pandemic could lead to an intensification of loneliness among Japanese middle-aged men
The number of suicides in Japan reached 21,077 in 2020, the first increase in 11 years, and even 2.5 times the number of deaths from COVID-19.
During the pandemic, people who were 95% close to suicide faced new challenges.
Okamoto Junko recommends creating weak connections to prevent a situation where a person has no one to talk to for many months and years
Japan's economic situation has deteriorated due to the impact of the coronavirus epidemic.
Economic factors seem to play a role in the rise in female suicides in 2020, according to Roko Ueda, an associate professor at the Institute of Political Economy at Waseda University in Japan.
15 percent of residents in Japan do not communicate with people outside the family circle at all.
For the entire period of 2019, three people suffered from suicide, for the nine months of 2020, these children have already 10.
The social and economic consequences of the pandemic have also led to mental health problems for many Japanese.
The family environment in Japan has changed due to the coronavirus epidemic.
The Ministry of Health, Labor and Welfare has a Suicide Prevention Program to focus on children and adolescents, the elderly and women.
The number of suicides among women in Japan increased by almost 15% in 2020.
A Japanese mother expressed her fear of killing her own child due to emotional instability and sought help on an online counseling platform.
Alcohol can facilitate communication, and the hostess of an institution can play the role of an intermediary and stimulate communication between visitors
The Palestinians have expressed fierce protests and refused to recognize the agreement

The Palestinian self-government insists that the so-called "Arab consensus" is valid, that is, only if Israel meets certain conditions will Arab countries establish diplomatic relations with Israel, including the need for Israel to recognize the Palestinian state with East Jerusalem as its capital.
The struggle of the nations of the region against the Zionist enemy will continue

The agreement signed last week between the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain and Israel was a betrayal of the Palestinian cause and a severe blow to their struggle for an independent Palestinian state in the occupied territories.
The rulers of Abu Dhabi have been playing games with the internal Palestinian issue for years.
Ismail Qaani said: "Iran is ready to confront the dangers of the deal of the century alongside the Palestinian people and especially the resistance forces."

Palestinian Authority Prime Minister Mohammed Ashtiyah pointed to the agreement between the United Arab Emirates and Israel to normalize relations and halt the plan for the annexation of the Palestinian territories.
Palestinian leaders have aligned with the Turkish and Iranian governments against Arab states in the region, according to Bandar bin Sultan.

Bassam al-Salehi, another member of the PLP and secretary general of the Palestinian People ’ s Party, said: “The UAE ’ s position is to stab the Palestinian people in the back and no one will be fooled by this demagogic claim to stop annexation. ”

Palestinian leaders called for demonstrations outside the Israeli occupied territories and the embassies of the United States, Israel, Bahrain and the Arab League to protest what they called a "shameful agreement".
The normalization of relations between Israel and the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain has weakened the common position of the Arab countries as a whole on the Palestinian issue.
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas condemned the move and said that "peace, security and stability in the region will not be achieved unless Israel ends its occupation."
The Trump administration's Middle East peace plan allows Israel to annex settlements on Palestinian land.
Taiwan is the first choice for many overseas Chinese and foreign people to perform plastic and cosmetic surgery.

More than 90% of foreign patients admitted to hospitals in Iran are from countries in the region including Afghanistan, Iraq, Azerbaijan, Kuwait, Oman, Bahrain and other countries in the Persian Gulf region.
Cosmetic procedures have increased in clinics in the United States, Japan, South Korea and Australia during the Corona period.
Due to the working conditions and staying at home and even covering their face, many people are more willing to do any beautiful action.

It is necessary to inform the public about the serious and irreparable complications of some cosmetic surgeries, especially in non-reputable clinics and by doctors who are not specialized in cosmetic surgery.
Elderly people can also be candidates for cosmetic surgery if general anesthesia does not pose a risk to them
Working women and housewives, for various reasons that they are no longer in gatherings, seize the opportunity and submit to these measures.
The "Korean Wave" has led to a huge increase in the medical tourism industry in South Korea, especially the plastic surgery industry.
The number of foreigners receiving treatment in Korean cosmetic hospitals increased by 968 compared with the previous year to 48,849.
Cosmetic surgery is considered a normal occurrence in countries such as Brazil, the United States, and South Korea.

The rapid barbeque fever and thirst for the resemblance of Hollywood stars in the face, smile and body has led to the black market in the field of all kinds of beauty surgeries and many underground specialized centers for skin and hair beauty and barbeque shaving to form.
Iran is one of the most reputable countries in the field of plastic and cosmetic surgery in the region.
The airfare, hotel and hospital surgeries all together cost a total of $3,800 for Meryl.
Dental tourism is growing rapidly and millions of people travel from countries such as the United Kingdom, the United States and Canada to get affordable dental care.
The "Brazilian hip lift" operation involves extracting fat from the abdomen or other parts and injecting it into the buttocks to create a more perfect hip shape.
Chinese tourists can travel to Iran and have a treatment and surgery program at the same time.
A square with a playground, a zone for sports, and a place for walking dogs will be built on Mashinostroitelnaya street.
The parks range from 300 to 1500 square meters
The park features creative environmental protection devices.
The creation of neighborhoods and pocket parks in different areas of cities can help protect the rights of citizens and children.
New Taipei City Xindian District Qiaoren Park game area access lines are barrier-free and friendly design, and enjoy sufficient rest and leisure space.
In Hongkou District, a pocket park was built after a number of illegal buildings were demolished.
The participatory budget concept allows residents and children to jointly plan a special park.

The combination of digital and innovative technologies with existing urban planning can help cities better manage the mobility of the commuter population and the poor, and reduce vulnerability to future climate impacts.
The survey found that the most common types of public space in Wan Chai District are small public spaces or "pocket parks", such as sitting-out areas or gardens.
The project aims to improve the quality of life of residents
Ge Zhaonian, director of the Forest Protection Section of the Resilient Urban Forest Project, pointed out that the project can increase the urban green area, plant diversity and improve soil quality.
Gorgan Municipality will take 97 steps to build and operate the 12 pocket parks.
The city council has special planning in the area of sustainable urban green space development, especially in the less affluent areas of the city.
Neighborhood parks are a factor in improving city life.
The problem of lack of green areas is especially acute in the “sleeping areas” of Kiev.
Low-carbon urban transformation in the post-pandemic period will help address climate change and increase resilience as the best path to economic recovery, according to the Hepburn and Stern report.
Socializing and playing in equipped and secured amusement centers and parks reduces life tensions and increases the enjoyment and satisfaction of life.
A flowerbed with a bridge will be built in the "pocket square" on Enthusiasts street to make it interesting for children to play.
A small green space of only 500 square meters has been installed with three leisure seats and some small outdoor fitness equipment at the corner of Fuxing East Road and Zhonghua Road.
"Pocket squares" will be within walking distance for residents and will decorate the yards.
The park will have facilities such as children’s play space, cycling track, fountain and flower box.
The development and construction of neighborhood parks and urban green spaces aims to create and develop the spirit of social vitality and increase social interactions among citizens.
Taiwan Housing Group Trend Center Manager Jiang Yihui said that the three big parks with the highest house price increase have a common point of green space of 5 ~ 6 hectares.
The park provides an opportunity for children to gain food and agricultural knowledge.

Developing countries are experiencing rapid urban growth, and their cities' carbon reduction or resilient urban planning are seen as important measures to reduce their vulnerability to future climate impacts.
Wan Chai has the second lowest per capita public space in Hong Kong, with only 0.7 square meters per person.
The mayor of Tabriz considered the neighborhood parks very important in promoting social, economic and environmental indicators of urban life.
The project creates artificial urban forests to enhance the city ’ s resilience, tolerance and resilience to environmental change.
The per capita green space and parks of Gorgan is 7.43 square meters per person based on the population of 368,000 people.
Gorgan Municipality’s approach is to increase urban green space per capita and the balanced distribution of amenities throughout the city.
Valiasr city has a high population density and the park is intended to provide a suitable context for recreation and entertainment of residents and families.
This is an attempt to instill in the Luhansk region the European trends of public park spaces.
Turkey has contributed more than 150 TV series to an estimated 500 million viewers in 146 countries around the world.
Watching Turkish series has affected part of Iranian lives, especially housewives.
Some cultural analysts considered the home theater network as a reasonable response to satellite networks and an opportunity to overcome their works.
The media monitoring center at the Press and Information Agency of Uzbekistan concluded that the series contradicts national mentality and traditions, as well as family values of Uzbeks.
TV critic Sina Kluoğlu in the Milliat newspaper added: "Advertising in this type of series is natural. The point is that it should be done cautiously."
Turkish TV series has been broadcast as the country’s soft power in recent years, especially on the television of Arab countries.
The concept of soft power was attributed to Joseph Nye, but the Ottoman Empire used soft power in its era to a large extent.
The US $40 million series Kingdoms of Fire, jointly funded by the two governments, was broadcast in 2019, highlighting the Ottoman Empire as the tyrant who has ravaged the Arab world for centuries.
In Iran, melodrama and family series have a large audience, and many examples can be found in such examples, including "Shahrzad" in the home show.
The Turkish TV series “The Queen is a songbird ” was liked by Russian viewers.
The ban on the Turkish TV series "Black Love" is seen as part of President Shavkat Mirziyoyev's broader effort to adopt a new approach to Islam.
“One Thousand and One Nights ” was exported to about 80 countries around the world in 2010.
Family affection, respect, and dignity among family members have been reduced by the impact of these series.
The cultural united front is a soft knife to dig the corner of the current system of Arab countries due to their lack of mature local cultural industries.
Turkish television series have reached South America after capturing the film market of the Indian subcontinent, the Middle East, the Caucasus, Central Asia and the Balkans.
The level of life and religious beliefs in the majority of Turkish society is different from the image displayed in these series.
Western culture is being imposed on Iranian homes through these series.
The suspension of the show was preceded by an article on the Islamic website Azon.uz, which threatened the actors with punishment for their involvement in the series.
Some critics discredited the series as a "propaganda" series.
Elements of Turkey’s soft power include the Turkish Cooperation and Coordination Agency, the Coordinator of Public Diplomacy, the Yunus Emre Institute, the Anadolu Agency, and Turkish Airlines.
Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s Justice and Development Party needed an ideology like neo-Ottomanism.
The series tells about the history of Muslim Oghuz Turks

New series of Turkish soap operas were very popular among viewers around the world: they are bought for hire even in Arab and Latin American countries - regions more accustomed to exporting their own "telenovelas".
Erdoğan authorities support political Islamic forces in other Middle Eastern countries, either explicitly or implicitly.
The series “Bride of Istanbul” reflects the controversial contrast of modern values with conservative traditions.
The website Azon.uz was criticized by Uzbek officials for violating the boundaries of what is allowed and for containing extremist appeals.
The channel is broadcast in many countries in the Middle East and North Africa, and is said to be broadcast in 24 countries with 18 different channels.

Turkish series are still popular today, and the purpose of the satellite here is not the Persian-language foreign networks, but the tendency of the Iranian audience among the satellite networks is more toward watching the Turkish series.
MOXIE can produce oxygen for future astronauts to breathe on Mars, and also need not drag a large amount of oxygen from Earth as a propellant in the future.
MOXIE was first tested on April 20 and produced more than 5 grams of oxygen for an hour of operation
Perseverance has an experimental installation MOXIE to receive oxygen from carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of Mars.
One ton of oxygen is enough for four astronauts for a year on Mars for breathing

A test vehicle for extracting oxygen from the Martian atmosphere, as well as a "space helicopter" that is set to fly for the first time on a planet other than Earth, are among those on the Perseverance rover.
This technology will solve a number of problems in the organization of manned flights to the planet.
MOXIE converts carbon dioxide into oxygen and carbon monoxide (CO)
Scientists have developed a method for producing oxygen and hydrogen from a liquid saline solution in Martian lakes using electrolysis.
The technology of extracting oxygen from the atmosphere of Mars will help astronauts produce rocket fuel on the planet.
MOXIE was launched on July 30, 2020, as part of the Mars 2020 mission.
5 grams of oxygen would be enough for one astronaut to breathe for about 10 minutes
Cyanobacteria, also known as blue-green algae, can successfully grow in Martian atmospheric conditions.
The source of energy will be able to keep the rover in working order for at least 14 years after the completion of the planned mission.
The atmosphere of Mars consists mainly of carbon dioxide (95 percent) and nitrogen (3 percent).
The demonstration aims to use an advanced version of the experimental instrument energy to contribute to further human exploration in the future.
NASA hopes that astronauts will land on Mars in 10 years

A team at Washington University in St. Louis has developed a new technology that uses electricity to break down salt water into oxygen and hydrogen, convert it into usable fuel and oxygen, and can operate in the minus 36 degrees Celsius Martian atmosphere.
MOXIE currently produces up to 10 grams of oxygen per hour.
A court in California ordered Monsanto to pay another victim with cancer 78 million dollars in October 2018.
Roundup weed killer has been sold in more than 160 countries.
The jury found it proven that the elderly couple contracted lymphoma due to the fact that for 30 years used a sprayer for weeds Roundup - this brand belongs to Bayer.
Monsanto developed glyphosate — a key ingredient in Roundup — in the 1970s.
The International Agency for Research on Cancer concluded that glyphosate is carcinogenic in 2015.
Monsanto has been sued in the United States for suspected cancer caused by the herbicide Roundup, and Bayer is seeking to settle the lawsuit with $11 billion in damages.
Bayer has faced more than 100,000 lawsuits in the United States for its herbicide.
More than 13,400 similar lawsuits against Bayer have been filed in the US courts
Germany's Bayer company bought US rival Monsanto for $66 billion in 2018, and Monsanto's product Roundup was also part of the acquisition.
The company is ready to pay from $6 billion to $8 billion to settle claims.
The settlement is subject to approval by the San Francisco Court.

Bayer AG will announce that it has reached an approximately $10.5 billion agreement with plaintiffs in the United States who have accused its Roundup herbicide of causing cancer to settle tens of thousands of lawsuits.
Shares of the German corporation Bayer are falling by more than 2% amid news that a court in the United States awarded a fine of $2 billion to the company.
The agreement was made with an estimated 50,000 to 85,000 plaintiffs.
A lawsuit in 2019 found that a Roundup user, Edwin Hardeman, received $5.3 million in damages from Monsanto for cancer.
More than half of glyphosate produced in China is exported, including the Roundup herbicide that Monsanto manufactures and distributes globally.
Bayer has been accused by farmers of causing cancer by the Roundup herbicide product.
France voted against the extension of the permit for the use of glyphosate in Europe, and we are trying to mobilize other European states to share our position.
In Russia, Monsanto is present as a supplier of seeds and plant protection products, and the herbicide Roundup is widely available.
Bayer will sell some business groups, lay off about 12,000 employees and 3.3 billion euros in losses due to ongoing legal proceedings and other factors.
German pharmaceutical company Bayer said its U.S. company Monsanto production of herbicide "Roundup" reported cases of cancer in the United States in the second quarter of about 5,000 new cases.
The German company, which recently acquired Monsanto, is accused of producing genetically modified crops as well as pesticides and herbicides containing carcinogenic glyphosate.
There are 125,000 lawsuits alleging cancer caused by Bayer's herbicide Roundup.
Aimee Wagstaff, Jennifer Moore, and Brent Wisner collectively represent about 20,000 plaintiffs.
The company will pay $10.1 billion to $10.9 billion for the settlement.
Los Angeles Judge Olguin said the agreement was too broad and would exempt Bayer from future claims.
Bayer, the German chemical company, said on Wednesday that a legal dispute over cancer caused by its Roundup brand herbicide has reached a $2 billion settlement with regulators.
Since the first Roundup carcinogenicity conviction, Bayer has lost nearly 46% of its market value and its share price has fallen to 2012 levels.
As of the end of June, there were about 125,000 filed and unfiled Roundup-related claims.
Sublethal concentrations of roundup have toxic effects on gill structure and reproductive performance of male carp.
The painting was found in the courtyard of the gallery of modern art in the Italian city of Piacenza.

Italian prosecutor Ornella Chicca said the actual painting was verified as authentic, but it remains unclear whether the painting was later smuggled back into the wall mezzanine or was hidden there from the beginning, pending further examination and clarification.
Police are questioning the two men, who appear to be members of a large gang, after the local newspaper Libertà received a confession from the two men about the 1997 robbery.
The frame of the painting was thrown away on the roof of the gallery building to make it look like thieves broke the frame to pass the painting through the window overlooking the roof.
The painting was kept in the Ricci Oddi Gallery of Modern Art until it was stolen in preparation for a special exhibition on February 22, 1997.
The artwork was discovered in December while hiding in a plastic bag in one of the walls of the Ricci Odie Gallery in Piacenza.
A gardener was cleaning the ivy of the museum’s outer wall when he found a metal plate on the wall, and when he opened it, he found a canvas painting in a black sackcloth bag.
The painting is one of the last works by Gustav Klimt, painted in 1916-1917.
Doubts art critics in the authenticity of the find was caused by the fact that the picture remained in excellent condition, being, presumably, abandoned by robbers 22 years ago.
The painting is estimated at 60 million euros.

In the Italian city of Piacenza, a gardener peeled ivy off the wall in the Ricci Oddi gallery and accidentally found a painting by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a Woman ” stolen 22 years ago from the same gallery.
The thieves claim they returned the painting four years ago as a "gift to the city".
Portrait of a Lady, a $500 million painting, was stolen from the Ricci Oddi Gallery of Modern Art on 22 February 1997.
The painting is kept in a guarded safe by the Italian Central Bank, it remains at the disposal of the judiciary.
Prosecutors will test whether the painting has been hidden in the wall or has been returned recently, and the painting can be re-exhibited after completion.
The thieves had confessed to what they had done before, but no one believed them.
Thieves used a device similar to a fishing rod to remove the painting from the wall and took it out of the gallery through a skylight that had been removed from the glass.
Experts used ultraviolet light and other tests to compare the 1996 photo to confirm that the painting was genuine.
The ivy that covered the wall had not been cut in nearly 10 years, hiding the crack in the wall where the painting was found.
In 2014, the case was reopened, as the police found a match fingerprint on the frame with some other prints.
The painting found is indeed the “Portrait of a Lady ” by Gustav Klimt, which was stolen from the Ricci Oddi Gallery of Modern Art in 1997.
The thieves said in a letter to Ermanno Marian, an employee of the newspaper Libertà, that they stole the work of the Austrian artist worth 60 million euros in February 1997, and then returned it.
Italian collector Giuseppe Ricci Oddi bought the painting in 1925 and kept it in the Audi Gallery of Modern Art of the same name until it was stolen on February 22, 1997.
Last year’s high temperature in the summer led to a decrease in the harvest in Ukraine.
The yield and quality of crops in Latvia can be reduced due to uneven rainfall and extreme weather conditions.
The production of certain vegetables will fall by as much as 30%, pulses will be reduced by 30% to 60% in Africa, and up to 40% of banana-growing areas will no longer be available.
Rising temperatures affect crop yields and reduce nutritional values, such as rice, wheat, barley and potatoes, by 6% to 14%.
Global warming can give rise to hordes of hungry insect pests.
Climate change will affect the areas in which most of the crop is now grown.
Average temperatures at different times of the year will increase by 2.3-3.1 degrees.
Agriculture needs to be more integrated in response to climate change.
Climate change is a new threat to the vineyards.
Climate change affects water and soil penetration in many regions of the world.
Water retention capacity of the soil can be enhanced through natural farming methods.
More than 150 million people around the globe will be at risk of protein deficiency by 2050.
Genome editing technology, such as CRISPR/Cas9, allows for precise modification of genetic sequences and can help crops cope with climate change.
Climate change has positive implications for higher latitudes like northern Europe, but negative implications for tropical and subtropical regions.
The implementation of new irrigation systems plays an important role in saving and increasing water efficiency.

The United Nations report on the impact of climate change on agriculture released Thursday said that changes must be made from the use of farmland, the production of crops and human food consumption, otherwise it will be difficult to guarantee human food supply, maintain human health and maintain the diversity of the earth's species in the face of increasing global warming.
Reducing food waste can help reduce carbon dioxide emissions from the production of food.
A relative increase in temperature indicators and an increase in precipitation in the winter period allow plants of winter cereals and rapeseed to winter successfully in Ukraine.
Every year there will be less frosty days, and more hot days and tropical nights in Latvia.
The crops of rice, corn and wheat will be threatened.
Rainfall in Iran is decreasing.
Allocation of optimal cultivation pattern in the province is necessary due to the consequences of climate change.
Rising temperatures during the growing season are expected to be the biggest factor in the decline in agricultural productivity.

Warming will affect the natural enemies of insects, and they will be affected by other factors of climate change, such as changes in rainfall patterns or more frequent periods of extreme temperatures.
The extreme climate index has been rising.
Air pollution could lead to a global rice crisis.
Deforestation and poor farming practices can undermine the ability of soils to absorb carbon dioxide.
The United Nations has warned of an impending “catastrophe ” of famine that will affect 34 million people worldwide.
Climate change and global warming have caused irreparable damage to some countries in recent years.
Scientists are developing mathematical models to identify genetic material that could help improve the adaptability of crops to climate change.
The temperature rise has given cold countries the "warming benefit", while the temperature in hot countries is far away from the ideal temperature, and has been punished by the "warming penalty".
Agriculture uses 70% of the world’s water.

The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) recently released its Special Report on Climate Change and Land, highlighting the environmental impact of agriculture and the need to make full use of existing farmland to prevent further destruction.
Asghar Farhadi won the Golden Bear of the Berlin Film Festival in 2011 for "A Separation from Simin".
The film won an Amnesty International award.

Director Jafar Panahi was banned from filming because he was accused of endangering national security, but he secretly shot and transported the film "Taxi in Iran " (2015), which won the Golden Bear Award at the Berlin Film Festival.
The Golden Bear for Best Film of the 70th Berlinale goes to "Sheytan vojud nadarad" (There Is No Evil), directed by Mohammad Rasoulof.
The Berlin Film Festival has shown support for unofficial representatives of Iranian cinema and anti-Iranian works.
Iranian film Innocence wins Golden Bear at Berlin Film Festival
The film “The Devil Does Not Exist” recounts narratives about freedom under the pressure of coercion and deals with the death penalty in Iran.
In Iran, a film director was banned from making films and even imprisoned because the subject matter was not up to the government’s mind
Spokraman-Review described the rare separation from Simin Farhadi as a family story that presents a complex and honest picture of life in Iranian society.
Jafar Panahi's film “Three Faces” depicts a well-known actress receiving a shocking video of a young girl who can’t help but decide to live.
Mohammad Rasoulof was not allowed to leave Iran and still won with the film "The Devil Does Not Exist".
Asghar Farhadi won the Best Foreign Language Film Oscar in 2012 and 2017 for his films “A Farewell” and “The Salesman”.
Farhadi's films have won many awards at international film festivals, including the Cannes International Film Festival and the Berlin International Film Festival.
Jafar Panahi has been sentenced to 20 years of a travel ban, as well as a ban on filmmaking inside Iran.
The film "There Is No Evil", directed by Iranian dissident director Mohammad Rasoulof, won the Golden Bear Award for Best Picture at the Berlin Film Festival.
The film was present in the Berlin competition section and was the only representative of Iranian cinema at this year ’ s Berlin Film Festival.

The directors of the 71st Cannes Film Festival asked the authorities of Iran and Russia to allow the departure of Iranian and Russian directors Jafar Panahi and Kirill Serebrennikov to participate in the festival.
Iranian cinema has won three Golden Bears in a decade.
The film "The Devil Does Not Exist" is about four people who are assigned to carry out the death penalty, each making their own choice.
The taxi driver in "Night Watch" picked up young men and women who were protesters.
The chairman of the jury of the 70th Berlin International Film Festival was the famous British actor Jeremy Irons.
Mina, a thirty-five-year-old woman whose husband was killed for murder, is now living a difficult and miserable life
The film "Namo" by Nader Saivar is also present in the "Forum and Forum Expanded" section.
Mohammad Rasoulof was sentenced by an Iranian court to one year in prison, and banned from going abroad and participating in social activities for two years.
The English Channel is a dangerous route for refugees to reach Britain.
A new agreement has been signed between French Interior Minister Gerald Darmanin and British Interior Minister Priti Patel and will come into force in the coming days.
The British and French governments have taken special measures to tackle illegal crossings, but Paris has refused to accept migrants who have crossed the border.
French authorities on Tuesday reported the capsizing of a small boat carrying asylum seekers and the death of four people on board in the English Channel.

Five members of a family called Irannejad, including 9-year-old Anita Irannejad, 6-year-old Armin Irannejad and 15-month-old Artin Irannejad, died in the recent sinking of refugee boats in the English Channel.
About 300 boats have crossed the English Channel since the beginning of the year, and about four thousand migrants have landed on the English coast.

Many refugees and migrants who have made their way to Calais in France are among those trying to cross the Channel by boat, especially as police and border guards on roads, including in the Channel Tunnel, have increased surveillance, making it harder to hide in cars and trucks.
The boat was reportedly carrying 17 people but smugglers had 27 people on board, while the British Meteorological Office had previously warned of bad weather and high winds in the Channel region.
British officials confirmed that the 17 migrants, including three children, were able to reach British shores by hijacking a 12-meter fishing boat near the French port of Calais early Tuesday.
British Home Secretary Priti Patel said the number of French patrols will be doubled from December 1, and drones and radar will be used to detect any attempts to cross the sea.

Home Secretary Sajid David cut short his Christmas holiday with his family in Africa on Sunday and returned home to tackle a difficult situation with an unexpected wave of illegal migrants arriving in Britain by boat from France.
Last year, the number of people who made it to Britain in this way reached 8,461 for the entire year
Nearly 29,000 people applied for asylum in the UK last year, including more than 5,000 children, according to data from the Office of National Statistics.
Eight of the migrants, including two children, were rescued by the French navy after their boat’s engine failed 20 kilometers from Calais.
The British government says the new plan will create “safe and legal pathways” to seek asylum in the country.
The plight of migrants in French refugee camps led them to make such a perilous journey.
A significant number of the migrants attempting to cross the English Channel are Iranians, Afghans and Syrians
The Stratolaunch Model 351 aircraft has the possibility of carrying and launching from it in one flight up to three Pegasus XL launch vehicles.
The wingspan of the Stratolaunch aircraft is 117 meters.
The fuselage design of the world's largest aircraft is a bit unusual, with two fuselages and the middle of the plane empty.
The first flight lasted two and a half hours, during which it flew at 17,000 feet in the Mojave Desert, with a top speed of 189 miles per hour (304 km / h).
The pilots evaluated flight characteristics during the first flight.
The Talon-A aircraft can also be launched by being lifted to a high altitude with the help of a Stratolaunch Roc aircraft.
The Stratolaunch aircraft has a mass of 226.8 tons.
The aircraft is able to lift a cargo weighing more than 360 tons into the air
During tests, the Stratolaunch Model 351 was accelerated to a speed of 218 kilometers per hour.
Stratolaunch is powered by six Pratt & Whitney PW4056 engines.
The Stratolaunch aircraft made the first test flight on Saturday, April 13
Stratolaunch is planned to be used as an aerial platform for launching launch vehicles to deliver cargo to low Earth orbit.

The aircraft is expected to be an airborne launch platform to put small satellites into orbit, with the goal of providing the military, private companies and even NASA with a more affordable way to enter space.
The world ’ s largest aircraft took off for the second time.
Stratolaunch was created in 2011 by Paul Allen, one of the founders of Microsoft.
The company has also begun assembling a reusable version of the Talon-A, which is scheduled to begin testing in 2023.
The Stratolaunch Model 351 surpasses the wingspan of the Ukrainian An-225 Mriya.
The aircraft has a wingspan longer than an American football field, a double fuselage and six Boeing 747 engines.
The total flight time of the Roc's second test flight was 3 hours and 14 minutes.
Stratolaunch is developing a reusable Talon-A polar sonic aircraft with a maximum speed of 6 Mach.
The world’s largest aircraft has 28 wheels.
The Stratolaunch aircraft weighs 230 tons.
Technical characteristics of the aircraft allow it to carry a payload weighing up to 250 tons.
The new aircraft is ready for use.
Stratolaunch plans to carry future spacecraft to high altitude.
The aircraft can carry a 110-ton rocket from the ground to 35,000 feet in the stratosphere, and then fire the rocket, launch it out of the atmosphere, and then send satellites into low Earth orbit.
A fan with a poster provoked a mass accident at the Tour de France
The accident was caused by a fan who went out on the track with a poster to attract the attention of television viewers.
A woman with a poster tried to get into the frame in front of the peloton and was hit by a German cyclist Tony Martin.
One cyclist had to be removed from the race because of the injuries
French police have detained a 30-year-old French woman in connection with the case.
More than 20 participants were injured, and four had to withdraw from the race.
A woman in a yellow coat with a sign in German that reads “Grandma and Grandpa” was involved in the incident.

French military and police authorities announced that they would conduct an investigation into the incident, which resulted in involuntary injury due to a deliberate breach of safety obligations, and that it would be completed within three months.
Marc Soler suffered three fractures to his left arm and was forced to retire that night.
Another accident occurred with less than 10 kilometres left in the race, forcing Chris Froome and several drivers to retire.
Spanish cyclist Marc Soler received fractures of two hands, and Frenchman Cyril Lemoine – multiple fractures of the ribs and collapse of the lung.
The woman reportedly disappeared before the police arrived
The fan holding the sign seemed to be looking into the cameras, not at the approaching riders.
